2604.03498.title: Resource-Conscious Modeling for Next- Day Discharge Prediction Using Clinical Notes
2604.03498.paper_summary: Timely discharge prediction is essential for optimizing bed turnover and resource allocation in elective spine surgery units. This study evaluates the feasibility of lightweight, fine-tuned large language models (LLMs) and traditional text-based models for predicting next-day discharge using postoperative clinical notes. We compared 13 models, including TF-IDF with XGBoost and LGBM, and compact LLMs (DistilGPT-2, Bio_ClinicalBERT) fine-tuned via LoRA. TF-IDF with LGBM achieved the best balance, with an F1-score of 0.47 for the discharge class, a recall of 0.51, and the highest AUC-ROC (0.80). While LoRA improved recall in DistilGPT2, overall transformer-based and generative models underperformed. These findings suggest interpretable, resource-efficient models may outperform compact LLMs in real-world, imbalanced clinical prediction tasks.
2604.03498.paper_summary_zh: 及時出院預測對於優化選擇性脊椎手術單位的病床周轉和資源配置至關重要。這項研究評估了輕量級、經過微調的大型語言模型（LLMs）和傳統基於文本的模型在使用術後臨床筆記預測次日出院的可行性。我們比較了13個模型，包括使用XGBoost和LGBM的TF-IDF，以及通過LoRA微調的緊湊型LLMs（DistilGPT-2，Bio_ClinicalBERT）。使用LGBM的TF-IDF達到了最佳平衡，出院類別的F1分數為0.47，召回率為0.51，且AUC-ROC最高（0.80）。雖然LoRA提高了DistilGPT2的召回率，但整體上基於Transformer的生成模型表現不佳。這些發現表明，可解釋的、資源高效的模型在現實世界中不平衡的臨床預測任務中可能超越緊湊型LLMs。
2604.03498.author: Ha Na Cho et.al.
2604.03498.authors: Ha Na Cho, Sairam Sutari, Alexander Lopez, Hansen Bow, Kai Zheng
